full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: for the ablation study which parts of the algorithm should I ablate? theres the bernoulli gate, the projection, the auxiliary function choice... which ones make sense
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: The two clearest are: Bernoulli gate (turns it into NC) and projection (lets norm grow). These have clear theoretical roles.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Confirmed by looking at the algorithm structure — these are the two independent toggleable components.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: I want a single run_klr function with boolean flags for use_conservative and use_projection so I can toggle them independently. when use_conservative=False it should just update every step like NC. can you sketch the logic?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Outlined the toggle structure: if use_conservative compute p_t and Bernoulli, use h' gradient; else use l' gradient and always update. Implemented full function based on this.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Tested all 4 combinations of flags on a small dataset — behavior matches expectations.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: ablation 2 isnt working — removing projection shows literally zero difference whether I use R=20 or R=1. I think the norm never actually exceeds R because eta scales with R too. how do I make projection actually matter?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: eta = R/sqrt(T) means smaller R gives smaller eta so norm growth is proportional to R. Fix: decouple eta from R — keep the main eta but use tight R=2. This was a real debugging moment.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Printed the RKHS norm during training — confirmed it exceeds R=2 within the first ~10 updates when eta=0.94.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: bar chart comparing full model vs no-bernoulli vs no-projection — two subplots for accuracy and SV count
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Standard plt.subplots(1,2) with bars and text annotations. Made two separate figures since they use different R values.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Both plots render correctly, values match printed output.
